SEARCH
Package org.openapitools.codegen.config

Class WorkflowSettings

java.lang.Object
org.openapitools.codegen.config.WorkflowSettings
public class WorkflowSettings extends Object
Represents those settings applied to a generation workflow.

      public WorkflowSettings()
      Instantiates a new workflow settings.

  • Method Details

    • newBuilder

      public static WorkflowSettings.Builder newBuilder()

    • newBuilder

      public static WorkflowSettings.Builder newBuilder(WorkflowSettings copy)

    • getInputSpec

      public String getInputSpec()
      Gets input spec's location, as URL or file
      Returns:
      the input spec

    • getOutputDir

      public String getOutputDir()
      Gets the output dir (where we write generated files). Defaults to the current directory.
      Returns:
      the output dir

    • isVerbose

      public boolean isVerbose()
      Configures verbosity of generation. When true, more messages will be printed during generation.
      Returns:
      true if verbose mode, false otherwise.

    • isSkipOverwrite

      public boolean isSkipOverwrite()
      Indicates whether or not the existing files should be overwritten during the generation. This option is more rudimentary than defining patterns in .openapi-generator-ignore or isEnableMinimalUpdate().
      Returns:
      true if defaulting to overwriting files, false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • Ignore File Format

    • isRemoveOperationIdPrefix

      public boolean isRemoveOperationIdPrefix()
      Indicates whether or not to remove the prefix of operationId, e.g. config_getId to getId.
      Returns:
      true if the operation id prefix should be removed during generation, false otherwise.

    • isSkipOperationExample

      public boolean isSkipOperationExample()
      Indicates whether or not to skip examples defined in the operation.
      Returns:
      true if the examples defined in the operation should be skipped.

    • isLogToStderr

      public boolean isLogToStderr()
      Indicates whether or not the generator's executor will write all log messages (not just errors) to STDOUT. Useful for piping the JSON output of debug options (e.g. -DdebugOperations) to an external parser directly while testing a generator.
      Returns:
      true if the executor should attempt to write all messages to stderr, false otherwise (which defaults to logging configuration, not necessarily stdout).

    • isValidateSpec

      public boolean isValidateSpec()
      Indicates whether or not generation should also validate an input specification.

      NOTE: Invalid specs may result in a generation error, disabling may cause unexpected results.

      Returns:
      true if spec document validation is enabled, otherwise false. Default: true.

    • isEnablePostProcessFile

      public boolean isEnablePostProcessFile()
      Indicates whether or not file post-processing is enabled for generators which support it. Refer to individual generator documentation for details.

      In general, once enabled, a generator will evaluate a command stored in LANG_POST_PROCESS_FILE.

      For example: export SCALA_POST_PROCESS_FILE=/usr/local/bin/scalafmt

      Here, a Scala generator which supports file post-processing will run scalafmt for each generated file.

      Returns:
      true if file post-processing is enabled, otherwise false.

    • isEnableMinimalUpdate

      public boolean isEnableMinimalUpdate()
      Indicates whether or not the generation should update only those files which have changed.
      Returns:
      true if minimal updates are enabled, otherwise false.

    • isGenerateAliasAsModel

      public boolean isGenerateAliasAsModel()
      Indicates whether or not the generation should convert aliases (primitives defined as schema for use within documents) as models.
      Returns:
      true if generate-alias-as-model is enabled, otherwise false.

    • isStrictSpecBehavior

      public boolean isStrictSpecBehavior()
      Indicates whether or not 'MUST' and 'SHALL' wording in the api specification is strictly adhered to. For example, when false, no automatic 'fixes' will be applied to documents which pass validation but don't follow the spec.
      Returns:
      true if the generator should attempt to strictly follow rules in the specification, otherwise false.

    • getTemplateDir

      public String getTemplateDir()
      Gets the directory holding templates used in generation. This option allows users to extend or modify built-in templates, or to write their own.
      Returns:
      the template dir

    • getTemplatingEngineName

      public String getTemplatingEngineName()
      Gets the name of the templating engine to target. This option allows a user to target an engine which differs from the generator default, or to refer to their own fully qualified type name of a custom template engine adapter.
      Returns:
      the templating engine name
      See Also:
      • Custom Engines

    • getIgnoreFileOverride

      public String getIgnoreFileOverride()
      Gets the override location for the .openapi-generator-ignore file. Most useful on initial generation.
      Returns:
      the ignore file override

    • getGlobalProperties

      public Map<String,String> getGlobalProperties()
      Gets system properties applied to the generator.
      Returns:
      the system properties

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

      Overrides:
